title: Triggers
description: Learn how to create triggers to respond automatically to game events

sections.title: What Are Triggers?
sections.content:
Triggers automatically respond when specific text appears in the game output. They monitor the MUD server's messages and execute commands when a match is found.

For example, when you see 'You are hungry', a trigger could automatically send 'eat bread'.

sections.title: Substring Matching
sections.content:
Triggers use **case-sensitive substring matching**:

- The trigger fires when the match text appears anywhere in the output
- Matching is case-sensitive: 'You' does not match 'you'
- Partial matches work: 'hungry' matches 'You are hungry'

Example:
- Match: 'You are hungry'
- This will also match: 'You are very hungry', 'You are extremely hungry', etc.

sections.title: Cooldown Period
sections.content:
The cooldown prevents triggers from firing too frequently:

- Set in milliseconds (1000ms = 1 second)
- After a trigger fires, it won't fire again until the cooldown expires
- Recommended: 2000-5000ms for typical triggers

Example:
- Match: 'You are hungry'
- Action: 'eat bread'
- Cooldown: 5000 (5 seconds)

This prevents spamming commands if the game repeats the message.

sections.title: Creating a Trigger
sections.content:
To create a trigger:

1. Go to Connection Settings
2. Navigate to Triggers
3. Click 'Add Trigger'
4. Enter the text to match (the pattern)
5. Enter the command to send when matched
6. Set the cooldown in milliseconds
7. Click Save

Your trigger is active immediately.

sections.title: Trigger Actions
sections.content:
Each trigger executes a single command when matched. To send multiple commands, use command chaining with semicolons:

Example:
- Match: 'You are hungry'
- Action: 'eat bread;drink water'

This sends both commands in sequence when the trigger fires.

sections.title: Using Variables in Triggers
sections.content:
Variables work in trigger actions:

**User variables ($):**
- Match: 'You are hungry'
- Action: 'eat ${food}'

This sends 'eat bread' if food=bread.

**System variables (%):**
- Match: 'You welcome $CHARACTER'
- Action: 'say Thanks for the welcome, %CHARACTER!'

This uses the character name in the response.

sections.title: Using Aliases in Triggers
sections.content:
You can invoke aliases directly in triggers using @:

**Direct alias invocation (@):**
- Match: 'You attack the goblin'
- Action: '@attack'

This invokes the 'attack' alias.

This is useful when you want triggers to use complex alias logic.

sections.title: Using Conditionals in Triggers
sections.content:
You can use #IF conditionals in trigger actions:

Example:
- Match: 'You see a monster'
- Action: '#IF $auto_attack=ON kill $target #ENDIF'

The command only fires if the condition is true.

sections.title: Client-Side Execution
sections.content:
Important: Triggers execute client-side only:

- They run in your MUDPuppy client
- They don't modify the game state directly
- Some games may not respond as expected to automated commands
- Some MUDs prohibit or limit automation

Use triggers responsibly and check the game's rules regarding automation.

sections.title: Troubleshooting Triggers
sections.content:
If a trigger isn't firing:

1. **Check if enabled** - Triggers can be toggled on/off
2. **Verify the match text** - Make sure it matches exactly (case-sensitive)
3. **Check cooldown** - Wait longer than the cooldown period
4. **Test with Echo** - Enable trigger echo to see matches in the terminal
5. **Check variables** - Make sure referenced variables exist

Use the 'Add Example' button in the Trigger Editor for ready-to-use trigger templates.
